label: SSG Marcus Johnson — Marine Corps, Camp Lejeune, Other Than Honorable Discharge, Rifleman

dd214.name: Johnson, Marcus T.
dd214.decorations: Sea Service Deployment Ribbon, Good Conduct Medal (2), National Defense Service Medal
dd214.dutyLocations: Marine Barracks Camp Lejeune NC, Marine Air Ground Task Force School Camp Lejeune NC, USS Saipan LHA-2
dd214.remarks: Eleven years active duty service with primary assignment at Camp Lejeune (1975-1986). Resided on base with family quarters. Separated under Other Than Honorable characterization due to administrative action. Medical records document multiple health issues potentially related to Camp Lejeune water contamination.

vaRecords.provider: VA Eastern North Carolina Health Care System
diagnosis: History of bladder cancer (C67 — 2001 status post radical cystectomy)
vaRecords.notes: Veteran served at Camp Lejeune 1975-1986, full 11 years including family quarters residence. VA presumptive conditions evaluation completed. History of bladder cancer (removed 2001) and current aplastic anemia consistent with contaminated water exposure (TCE, benzene).
CBC shows pancytopenia with borderline low hemoglobin (10.2 g/dL), WBC 3.2K, platelets 95K. Bone marrow biopsy shows hypocellularity. Treatment initiated with immunosuppressive therapy.

presumptiveExposures: Camp Lejeune water contamination (TCE, benzene, vinyl chloride) — 1975-1986
summary.functionalImpact: Aplastic anemia causes fatigue limiting work capacity. Frequent medical appointments required. Post-cystectomy continent urinary diversion requires ongoing management.
